message:
fix(messaging/redis): cancel-safe pooled connection management

Wrap all pooled connection acquisitions in DiscardOnDropGuard immediately
at acquire time, so that if any future is dropped mid-protocol-exchange
(timeout, select!, task cancellation), the desynced connection is
discarded rather than returned to the pool. Previously, cmd_bytes(),
Pipeline::exec(), and Transaction methods had gaps where a cancellation
between acquire and guard creation would leak a desynced connection.

Key changes:

1. cmd_bytes(): wrap in DiscardOnDropGuard; return_to_pool() on success
   or Redis-level errors (protocol still synced); auto-discard on I/O
   errors via Drop

2. Pipeline::exec(): wrap immediately; remove redundant manual
   ensure_initialized error handling (guard handles it); return_to_pool()
   after all responses read

3. Transaction: wrap in DiscardOnDropGuard during begin(), cmd(), exec(),
   and discard() — each method takes ownership of the raw connection,
   wraps it, and only defuses/returns on success. Dropped mid-exchange
   futures trigger the guard's Drop path.

4. DiscardOnDropGuard::drop(): now calls stream.shutdown(Both) before
   discard() so the peer promptly sees EOF/RST instead of a half-live
   socket lingering until TCP keepalive or timeout

5. Transaction::drop(): same shutdown-before-discard pattern

6. WATCH/UNWATCH: converted to synchronous methods that always return
   Err(Protocol) since watch state is connection-scoped and a pooled
   client cannot guarantee the same connection for MULTI/EXEC

7. DiscardOnDropGuard::return_to_pool(): new convenience method that
   defuses the guard and explicitly returns the connection to the pool

Adds 4 new tests:
- watch_rejects_pooled_client_api
- unwatch_rejects_pooled_client_api
- cmd_cancellation_discards_pooled_connection
- transaction_begin_cancellation_discards_pooled_connection
- dropped_transaction_queue_future_fails_closed_and_discards_connection

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
